key_risks: Soft June payrolls (July 2) could trigger growth scare hitting cyclicals and financials
Bank Q2 earnings disappointment on NII or credit could reverse KRE/XLF strength
Mega-cap tech rebound would cause SPY benchmark to outrun defensive tilt
Sticky core PCE (3.4%) prompts hawkish July 29 FOMC, pressuring rate-sensitive sectors
Biotech reversal risk after +17% monthly run-up

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent leadership with defensive characteristics; low beta (0.37) offers downside protection amid tech weakness while momentum is positive.
Big bank Q2 earnings (JPM, WFC on July 14) as key July catalyst; sector showing strong breadth with +6.88% 30d return and low drawdown.
ISM Manufacturing PMI at 53.3 with new orders 56.0 supports cyclical strength; broad rotation into non-tech cyclicals evident.
Captures market breadth rotation away from mega-cap tech; lower concentration risk with beta 0.74.
Strongest 30d momentum (+17.27%) with healthcare sector tailwind; catalyst-rich July with clinical data flow.

portfolio_rationale: Positioning for continued rotation from mega-cap tech (which dragged Nasdaq -0.7% on July 1) into defensive-cyclical breadth. Healthcare and Financials offer catalyst exposure (bank earnings) with lower volatility than tech. Sticky core PCE at 3.4% and Fed on hold argues against duration; equal-weight and cyclicals benefit from broader participation.
rationale_summary: Overweight healthcare, financials, and broad-breadth cyclicals to capture July rotation and bank earnings catalysts while avoiding concentrated mega-cap tech drawdown risk.
